title: Wonderfully Buttery and Snappy Australian Beef Tagliere
description: Indulge in the rich flavors and tender textures of this Australian-inspired beef Tagliere, featuring sour cream and fermented elements, all carefully prepared in a Buddhist-temple-friendly environment.

ingredients.name: Picanha beef
ingredients.notes: Choose grass-fed beef for optimal flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: Sour cream
ingredients.notes: Use full-fat sour cream for maximum richness.

ingredients.name: Fresh thyme
ingredients.notes: Chopped fresh thyme adds a bright, herbaceous note.

ingredients.name: Coarse sea salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for added texture.

ingredients.name: Australian bush tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Chopped tomatoes add a sweet, tangy element.

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Garnish with chopped parsley for added color.

ingredients.name: Grated Parmesan cheese
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality Parmesan for maximum flavor.

instructions: Season the picanha beef with coarse sea salt and freshly ground black pepper.
In a large skillet, heat the unsalted butter over medium-high heat until melted and slightly browned.
Sear the picanha beef for 2-3 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the beef to a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
While the beef is cooking, whisk together the sour cream, fermented chili sauce, and chopped fresh thyme in a small bowl.
Once the beef is cooked, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
To assemble the Tagliere, arrange the sliced beef on a large platter or individual plates, spooning the sour cream mixture over the top.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, Australian bush tomatoes, and grated Parmesan cheese.
Drizzle with truffle oil, if using.
